name: Abdullah ibn Muslim
title: First Hashemite Martyr
shortDescription: The son of Muslim ibn Aqeel and Ruqayyah (daughter of Imam Ali AS) who was the first from Bani Hashem to enter the battlefield after the Ansar.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Muslim ibn Aqeel was the brave son of Karbala's martyred ambassador (Muslim ibn Aqeel) and Ruqayyah (the daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali AS). He was raised in a family where bravery and sacrifice were woven into their blood.

On the day of Ashura, after all the non-Hashemite companions (Ansar) were martyred, it was the turn of the Prophet's household (Bani Hashem). According to many historical texts, Abdullah ibn Muslim was the first among the Hashemite youths to seek permission from Imam Hussain (AS) to enter the battlefield.

Despite his young age, he entered the battlefield with a warrior's spirit and recited epic poetry warning the enemies: 'Today I will meet my father Muslim and the chivalrous men who were killed in the path of the Prophet's religion.'

Abdullah fought valiantly and, in three successive attacks, took down a number of enemy forces. Ultimately, 'Amr ibn Subayh al-Saydawi' shot an arrow at him. Abdullah raised his hand to protect his forehead, but the arrow nailed his hand to his forehead. Then they attacked him with spears and swords, unjustly martyring him.
quote: (In his war poetry:) Today I will meet my father Muslim...
